questions.q: Who founded the Servants of India Society?
questions.explanation: The Servants of India Society was founded by Gopal Krishna Gokhale.

questions.q: In which year was the Servants of India Society founded?
questions.explanation: The Servants of India Society was founded in 1905.

questions.q: What was the main purpose of the Servants of India Society?
options: To train dedicated workers for national service
questions.explanation: The society trained dedicated workers to advance education, social reform, and constitutional progress.

questions.q: The Servants of India Society represented which strand of the early nationalist movement?
questions.explanation: The society embodied the moderate, service-oriented strand of the early nationalist movement.

questions.q: Members of the Servants of India Society took a vow to dedicate their lives to what?
questions.explanation: Members pledged themselves to a life of service to the nation.

questions.q: Which of these was a key area of work for the Servants of India Society?
questions.explanation: The society worked to advance education, social reform, and constitutional progress.

questions.q: The Servants of India Society was founded in which Indian city?
questions.explanation: Gokhale established the Servants of India Society in Poona (present-day Pune).

questions.q: The Servants of India Society sought political progress through which kind of methods?
questions.explanation: The society pursued constitutional and peaceful means in keeping with its moderate outlook.

questions.q: The Servants of India Society is best described as which kind of organization?
questions.explanation: It was a reform-minded public-service society dedicated to national upliftment.

questions.q: The Servants of India Society's founder was also a leading figure in which political body?
questions.explanation: Gokhale, the society's founder, was a leading moderate of the Indian National Congress.

questions.q: The headquarters of the Servants of India Society was established in which city?
questions.explanation: The society was headquartered in Poona, where Gokhale founded it in 1905.

questions.q: Members of the Servants of India Society were expected to accept what kind of personal lifestyle?
options: A simple life on modest allowances
questions.explanation: Members lived simply on modest allowances, devoting themselves wholly to public service.

questions.q: Which prominent later leader served as a member and second president of the Servants of India Society?
questions.explanation: V. S. Srinivasa Sastri was a distinguished member who succeeded Gokhale as president of the society.

questions.q: The Servants of India Society aimed to prepare a cadre of trained workers often described as 'national missionaries' devoted to what?
questions.explanation: The society sought to create 'national missionaries' committed to selfless public and constructive work.

questions.q: The Servants of India Society undertook relief and welfare work especially among which groups?
options: The poor, depressed classes, and tribal communities
questions.explanation: The society engaged in welfare work among the poor, depressed classes, and tribal communities.

questions.q: After Gokhale's death in 1915, the Servants of India Society continued to function under whose leadership?
questions.explanation: Following Gokhale's death in 1915, leadership passed to members such as Srinivasa Sastri.

questions.q: The Servants of India Society reflected which broad political philosophy of its founder?
options: Gradual reform and self-government within the British system
questions.explanation: It reflected Gokhale's moderate philosophy of gradual reform and self-government within the British framework.

questions.q: Members of the Servants of India Society devoted themselves to causes including the spread of which among the masses?
questions.explanation: Promoting education and literacy among the masses was a central concern of the society.

questions.q: The Servants of India Society sought to advance which type of progress alongside social reform?
questions.explanation: The society pursued constitutional progress toward self-rule together with social reform.

questions.q: The Servants of India Society can be seen as an institutional expression of which leader's lifelong ideals?
questions.explanation: The society institutionalized Gokhale's ideals of disciplined, selfless national service.

questions.q: How did members of the Servants of India Society generally view participation in active mass agitation in its early years?
options: With caution, preferring constructive constitutional work
questions.explanation: In line with their moderate outlook, members preferred constructive constitutional work over mass agitation.

questions.q: The training of workers in the Servants of India Society typically lasted for a fixed probationary period of about how long?
questions.explanation: New members underwent a probationary training period of roughly five years before full membership.

questions.q: The Servants of India Society's later president Srinivasa Sastri was widely admired for his command of which language and oratory?
questions.explanation: Srinivasa Sastri was celebrated for his eloquent command of the English language.

questions.q: The Servants of India Society published a weekly journal to spread its ideas; it was titled what?
questions.explanation: The society published a weekly journal called 'The Servant of India.'

questions.q: Members of the Servants of India Society took a pledge that included putting the country's interests above which of the following?
questions.explanation: The members' pledge required placing national interest above personal, communal, and class interests.

questions.q: The Servants of India Society undertook notable famine and flood relief work in which early-20th-century period of crisis?
options: During famines and natural calamities in India
questions.explanation: The society organized relief during famines and natural calamities affecting India.

questions.q: Hriday Nath Kunzru, a long-serving leader of the Servants of India Society, was based largely in which region of work?
questions.explanation: Hriday Nath Kunzru was a prominent member active largely in North India and a later guiding figure of the society.

questions.q: Although Gandhi admired Gokhale deeply, why did Gandhi ultimately not become a formal member of the Servants of India Society?
options: He was barred by the British
Some members had reservations about his methods
questions.explanation: Despite his reverence for Gokhale, Gandhi did not join, partly because some members had reservations about his approach.

questions.q: The Servants of India Society's emphasis on disciplined cadres of full-time workers influenced the later organizational model of which kind of body?
options: Modern Indian social-service and political organizations
questions.explanation: Its model of disciplined, full-time workers influenced later Indian social-service and political organizations.

questions.q: The Servants of India Society was founded shortly before which major political event that intensified nationalist feeling in Bengal?
questions.explanation: The society was founded in 1905, the same year as Curzon's Partition of Bengal, which inflamed nationalist sentiment.
